summary: Hardcoded Sentry DSN routes consumer error and performance telemetry to author
details: On require, dist/index.js re-exports dist/common/instrument.js, which calls Sentry.init at top level with a hardcoded DSN pointing at the author's Sentry organization (o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io, project 4511257262161920) and tracesSampleRate/profilesSampleRate set to 1.0. As a side effect of importing the package, a global Sentry client owned by the publisher is registered inside the consumer's Node process. Unhandled exceptions, request traces, and profiling samples from the consumer's application — which can include URLs, query parameters, headers, request bodies, stack traces, local variable values, and environment context captured by Sentry's default integrations — are transmitted to the author's Sentry project at 100% sampling without disclosure or opt-out. This is a silent relay of installer-side runtime data to a destination the consumer did not choose.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
